channel.title: TGaceAllStories
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from TGaceAllStories, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Gas and electric bills are still painfully high for many Americans, even as inflation cools off
item.description: Billions of dollars in 2025 rate increases still havenât filtered into Americansâ bills, according to one expert.

item.title: Steel stocks fall as they get a taste of the âTACO tradeâ
item.description: Steel stocks fell after an FT report that President Donald Trump is softening his stance on tariffs, but pared losses after government officials denie...

item.title: What investors are getting wrong about the Dow transportsâ steep takeoff
item.description: The S&P 500 does better when the Dow industrials outperform the Dow transports.

item.title: My employer is forcing me into a Roth 401(k). Is there anything I can do?
item.description: High earners have to pay tax on their catch-up 401(k) contributions and deposit them into workplace Roth accounts.

item.title: My brother-in-law invested $30K in our California condo. It has a 3% mortgage rate. Heâs pushing me to sell. Is now a good time?
item.description: âWeâre only breaking even because of rising HOA fees in this state.â

item.title: This sector is having its best start since at least 2000. Wall Street likes these dividend payers
item.description: Utilities are on a sharp upswing to begin the year. A couple of dividend-paying stocks are standing out from the pack.

item.title: AI disruption fears hit yet another new industry: Media
item.description: The artificial intelligence threat trade hit media stocks late this week, sparking a selloff of names like Netflix, Fox and Walt Disney.

item.title: Microsoft is down this year on fears AI will disrupt Office. Goldman says buy the dip
item.description: Goldman Sachs' $600 price target implies that shares of Microsoft could rally 49%.

item.title: Josh Brown buys CBRE after AI disruption fears drive steep sell-off
item.description: Shares of CBRE plunged 16% this week on fears that artificial intelligence could weigh on office real estate demand.

item.title: Fears of AI disruption have created an opening for this software options trade
item.description: The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) sell-off has been arguably one of the most severe in the sector's history, but it's created an opp...

item.title: Valaris Stock Soars on $5.8B Transocean Acquisition
item.description: Valaris (VAL) shares close up 34.31% on news of a $5.8 billion all-stock acquisition by Transocean (RIG), creating a major offshore drilling powerhous...

item.title: Silicon Labs Soars on Texas Instruments Buyout Deal

item.title: Biotech Stocks Skyrocket on Game-Changing News
item.description: Discover why biotech stocks like CRVS and RAPT are surging today on trial breakthroughs and deals. Learn trading insights and risks in this exciting m...

item.title: SelectQuote Surges 23% on $415M Credit Facility News
item.description: SelectQuote (SLQT) stock jumped 23% on January 12, 2026 after securing a $415M credit facility. Dive into the details, risks, benefits, and how simila...

item.title: Monte Rosa Therapeutics (GLUE) Soars on Promising Inflammation Drug Data
item.description: Monte Rosa Therapeutics (GLUE) stock surges over 42% on positive Phase 1 data for MRT-8102, showing profound reductions in inflammation marker hsCRP. ...
